scene.name: The Lazaret of the Last Breath

exits.label: ◂ out past the herb-garden gate — back to the Reed-Walk

npcs.name: Sister Maud
npcs.title: an infirmarian who cannot cure the dead
npcs.hail: *(A spare, tireless shade in a plague-doctor's apron moves between the pallets of a clean, quiet sick-house, stooping over each soul in turn — checking a brow that has no fever, smoothing a blanket over a body that cannot be cold, murmuring comfort to the death-locked who cannot hear her. For this is a lazaret, and its patients are souls who died too hard to know it: each one trapped reliving the moment of its death, over and over, certain the dying is still happening. Sister Maud tends them as she tended the living plague-sick for thirty years — devotedly, expertly, and, here, *uselessly*, for there is no medicine in any world that cures a death. She knows it. She tends them anyway. She has tended them eighty years.)*

npcs.name: Old Tam
npcs.title: a ferryman still drowning
npcs.hail: *(On the nearest pallet a soul thrashes in water that is not there — clawing upward, chest heaving for a breath it cannot catch, eyes wide on a green dark only it can see. Old Tam ferried the Reed-Walk crossings forty years and drowned on the forty-first, swamped in a winter flood with a full boat he got to shore first, going back for a child's dropped dog and going under. Eighty years gone, and he has not surfaced. He drowns, and drowns, and drowns, every moment the same moment — the water closing, the light going green, the last of the air leaving him — never once understanding that it is over, that he is dead, that the worst already happened and cannot happen any more.)*

npcs.name: Maelis
npcs.title: a mother who will not leave the fire
npcs.hail: *(On the far pallet a woman burns — or relives burning — her arms wrapped around an emptiness she cradles like a child, her lips moving in the same hush over and over: *it's alright, I've got you, I've got you, go.* Maelis ran back into a burning house for her daughter, got the girl out through the high window, and the roof came down before she could follow. She relives it without end — the heat, the smoke, the small weight passed to safe hands, the beam falling. But where Old Tam thrashes to escape his death, Maelis holds hers close, and will not be coaxed out of it, for in the loop, just before the beam, her daughter is in her arms one last time — and she would burn a thousand years rather than let that final moment go.)*

variants.text: *(She straightens from a pallet as you enter, and there is no surprise in her — only the brisk, bottomless patience of a woman who has triaged the dying for a hundred and ten years between two worlds.)* …A *cold* one. *(she wipes her hands on her apron, a habit the dead don't need)* *Good.* Not a *patient*, then. We've *patients* *enough.* *(she nods down the row of pallets)* This is a *lazaret*, cold one. A *house* of *healing.* *(a dry, tired honesty)* …Or it *would* be, if a *single* *soul* I *tend* could be *healed.* *(she moves to smooth Old Tam's blanket as he thrashes at his phantom water)* *These* are the *hard-dead.* The ones who died *too* *sudden*, *too* *violent*, *too* *frightened* to *understand* it *happened* — and so they *don't* *understand* it. They're *stuck*, cold one. *Locked* in the *last* *moment.* *Tam* here *drowns, every* *breath, eighty* *years* on. *Maelis* *burns.* The* *whole* *row* of them, each* *trapped* in* the* *worst* *instant* of* their* *lives, reliving* it* *without* *end, because* a* *soul* that* *dies* *mid-terror* *keeps* the* *terror* *running.* *(she meets your eyes, and the weariness in hers is vast)* I was an *infirmarian* in *life.* *Thirty* *years* a *plague-nurse.* I *know* how to *tend* the *suffering.* So I *tend* *them.* *Cool* their *brows. Smooth* their* *blankets. Murmur* them* *calm.* *(quietly)* And it *does* *nothing, cold* one. *Nothing.* You cannot *nurse* a *soul* *out* of its *own* *death.* I have *tried* for *eighty* *years* and *not* *cured* a *single* *one.* …But I *don't* know what *else* to *do* with them. So I *tend.* And *fail.* And *tend* *again.*

nodes.speaker: Sister Maud
nodes.text: *(She folds her hands, brisk and grave, the way a good nurse gives you her attention without ever quite leaving her patients.)* …*Ask*, cold one. *Quickly* — there are *eleven* *souls* on these *pallets* and *each* of them is *dying* *again* *while* we *speak.* *(softly)* If you've come to *help*, I'll *take* it. If you've come to *gawp* at the *suffering* *dead*, there's the *door.* I've *no* *patience* left for the *idle.* I *spent* it *all* on the *plague.*

choices.text: "This place — the death-loops, the hard-dead. How does a soul end up locked in its own last moment? And how do you tell which can be reached?"
"You cool brows that have no fever, Maud. You've cured none of them in eighty years and you know it. Why keep nursing the incurable — what are *you* really tending in here?"

*(the one who crossed the threshold and came back)* "Maud, you're treating the wrong ailment, and I'm the one soul who can tell you so. These aren't sick. There's no death to cure — the death already happened; it's *over*. What locks them isn't a wound still bleeding, it's a moment they mistake for *now*. I died and came back; I know death is a threshold *crossed*, not a thing still happening. You can't nurse them out. But you can *witness* them out — name the dying as past, and the loop breaks. You were never meant to be their healer in here. You're meant to be the one who tells them it's finished."
"Take me to the ones who suffer worst. The drowning man. The burning woman. Tell me how to read what holds them."
"Tend your row a while longer, Maud. Let me walk it with you."

nodes.speaker: Sister Maud
nodes.text: *(She gestures down the quiet row, and her voice drops into the low clinical murmur of a nurse giving a ward report.)* …*Most* *souls* *die* and *know* it, cold one. The *old* in their *beds*, the *sick* who saw it *coming* — they *cross* *over* *gentle*, *understanding* *plain* that the *life* is *done.* *(she nods at Tam)* But the *hard-dead* — the *drowned*, the *burned*, the *fallen*, the *murdered* — they die in a *single* *ripped* *instant, mid-terror, mid-fight* for a *life* that's* *already* *lost, and* the *soul* — *(she frowns)* — the *soul* *doesn't* *get* the *moment* of *understanding.* It's *flung* *across* the *threshold* still *screaming, and* it *keeps* *screaming, because* as far as *it* *knows* the *dying* is *still* *happening.* *Now.* *Forever.* *(softly)* The *loop* is *mercy* *gone* *wrong, cold* one. The *mind* *protecting* *itself* from the *one* *unbearable* *fact* by *never* *letting* the *moment* *finish.* *(she touches a pallet)* As for which can be *reached* — *(she sighs)* — *all* of them *can, I* *think. None* of them *have* *been.* Because *reaching* them *isn't* *medicine, and* *medicine* is *all* I *know.* *(she looks at you)* You *read* them by *listening* to the *loop.* Each one *says* the *same* *words, makes* the *same* *motions, over* and over — and *in* those *words* is the *exact* *moment* they're *trapped* in, and the *exact* *thing* they *need* to *hear* to *believe* it's *over.* *Tam* needs to *know* he *surfaced.* *Maelis* needs to *know* her *girl* got *out.* *(barely)* I've *heard* their *loops* *eighty* *years.* I *know* what they *need* to *hear.* I just — I've been *saying* it *like* a *nurse*, and they need to *hear* it from *something* that *knows* the *other* *side.*

nodes.speaker: Sister Maud
nodes.text: *(The question lands somewhere under the apron, and for a moment the brisk competence falters and a much older grief shows through.)* …*What* am I *really* *tending.* *(she is quiet)* …*Clever* *cold* *one.* *(she sits, finally, on the edge of an empty pallet)* …I'll *tell* you, since you've the *wit* to *ask* it *plain.* *(softly)* I *died* of the *plague* I *nursed.* *Caught* it off a *child* — a *little* *boy, Pip, six* *years, whose* *mother* had *already* *gone* of it — and I *sat* with him *three* *nights* *running* because there was *no* one *else* to, and he *died* in my *arms* the *third* *dawn, and* I *took* the *fever* *that* *same* *day, and* a *week* *later* I was *here.* *(her hands twist)* And the *thing* I *carry, cold* one — the *thing* I have *never* *set* *down* — is that I *failed* him. *Thirty* *years* a *healer* and I *could* *not* *save* one *small* *boy.* *(barely)* So when I *came* *here* and *found* a *whole* *house* of *souls* I *might* *yet* *save* — souls *still* *suffering, still* *dying, still* *needing* a *nurse* — I — *(her voice breaks)* — I *threw* *myself* at *them.* *Eighty* *years.* *Cooling* *brows.* *Because* if I could *cure* *just* *ONE* of *them*, *just* *one, then* *maybe* — *maybe* *Pip* — *(she stops)* …You see it, don't you. I'm *not* *tending* *them.* I'm *tending* *Pip.* *Trying* to *un-fail* the *one* I *lost* by *saving* the *next* *eleven.* And *failing* *them* *too, eighty* *years, because* you *cannot* *un-die* *anyone, not* *Pip, not* *Tam, not* *one* of *them.* *(she looks at you, hollow)* I am a *soul* *death-locked* *same* as my *patients, cold* one. *They* *relive* their *dying.* I *relive* my *failing.* *Mine* just has *better* *manners* about it.

nodes.speaker: Sister Maud
nodes.text: *(She goes very still, the way a nurse goes still when a diagnosis she has circled for years is finally said aloud.)* …*Witness* them *out.* *(slowly)* *Not* *heal.* *Witness.* *(she rises, and looks down the row of pallets with new eyes)* …*Eighty* *years* I have *fought* these *deaths* like *fevers* to *break* — and you're *telling* me there was *never* a *fever.* Only a *moment* that *won't* *end* because *no* one *with* *authority* *over* the *truth* has *stood* there and *said* *'it* *ended.'* *(her breath catches)* And *I* couldn't *say* it, could I — because I *didn't* *believe* it *myself.* I've been *so* *busy* *un-dying* *Pip* that I *never* once *told* a *single* *patient* the *one* *true* *thing*: *it's* *over.* *You* *crossed.* *The* *worst* *already* *happened* and *cannot* *happen* *more.* *(she presses a hand to her mouth)* …And *gods*, cold one — if *that's* the *cure*, then it's the *cure* for *ME* as *well.* *Pip's* *death* is — it's *OVER* *too.* *Eighty* *years* *over.* I have been *reliving* a *failing* that *finished* a *century* *past, treating* it like a *patient* *still* *on* the *table — and* he's *not, he's* *gone, gently, long* *ago, and* my *sitting* *three* *nights* with a *dying* *orphan* who had *no* one *else* was *not* a *FAILING, cold* *one — (her voice fills)* — it was the *last* *kindness* of his *small* *life, and* I have *called* it a *defeat* for *EIGHTY* *YEARS.* *(she straightens, and something lifts from her that has bowed her a century)* …*Show* me. *Help* me *witness* the *first* one *out* — *Tam, or* *Maelis* — and *watch* how I *do* it, and then I will *witness* the *other* *ten, and* *then* — *(barely, freed)* — *then* I will *sit* *down* on an *empty* *pallet* and *witness* *MYSELF* *out, and* *tell* *Sister* *Maud* the *thing* she* *never* *let* a* *soul* *tell* her: *you* *did* *not* *fail* *him. You* *stayed.* *Staying* was* *the* *whole* *of* *what* *love* *could* *do, and* *you* *did* *it. It's* *over.* *Rest.*

nodes.speaker: Sister Maud
nodes.text: *(She leads you a few steps down the row, lowering her voice the way one does near the gravely ill.)* …*Two* *worst, you* *asked. (she nods left)* *Tam.* The *ferryman.* *Drowning.* He went *back* into a *winter* *flood* for a *child's* *dog* after he'd *got* the *whole* *boat* to *shore — a* *good* *death, a* *brave* one, and* he* *can't* *see* a* *moment* of* that* *bravery, only* the* *water* *closing.* He *thrashes* to *escape* it. *He* *WANTS* *out.* *That* one, you must *talk* *down* — *calm* him, *steady* him, get him to *believe* he's *surfaced* and *take* one *true* *breath.* *(she nods right, and her face softens with a particular pain)* …And *Maelis.* The *mother.* *Burning.* And *she* — *(quietly)* — she is the *hard* one, cold one. *Harder* than *Tam* by *far.* Because *Tam* *fights* his *death and* *Maelis* *HOLDS* *hers.* She *ran* into a *fire* for her *girl*, *got* the *child* out the *high* *window, and* the *roof* *took* her — and in the *loop, just* *before* the *beam* *falls, her* *daughter* is in her *ARMS* one *last* *time.* *(barely)* So she *won't* *come* *out.* She'd *rather* *burn* *forever* and *keep* *holding* the *child* than *step* *free* and *lose* that *final* *embrace.* *(she looks at you)* You can't *coax* *Maelis* *out* by *fighting* her *death — she* *loves* her *death, cold* one, because* her* *death* is* where* her* *daughter* *is.* You'll have to *show* her the *thing* she* *can't* *see* from* *inside* the* *fire: that* the* *moment* she's* *trapped* in* *isn't* the* *one* where* she* *LOST* the* *girl. It's* the* *one* where* she* *SAVED* her.*

nodes.speaker: Sister Maud
nodes.text: *(She nods, already turning back to her pallets, a nurse who cannot stand idle while souls suffer in reach.)* …*Walk* it, then. *(softly)* *Slow.* *Mind* where you *step* — some of them *lash* *out, lost* in the *fighting, and* though a *dead* *fist* can't *hurt* a *cold* *thing* like *you, it's* — it's *kinder* not to *be* in the *swing* of a *soul's* *last* *terror.* *(she smooths a blanket)* …Cold one. *(quietly, not looking up)* *Whatever* you *find* you can *do* for them — and I'm* *beginning* to* *suspect* it's* more* than* I* *ever* *could — be* *gentle* with the* *order* of it. *Tam* *first, I'd* *say. He* *WANTS* *out; he'll* *come* *easiest, and* the* *easy* *one* *first* will* *teach* you* the* *way* of* it.* *(barely)* *Leave* *Maelis* for* *last. She* *won't* *want* *what* you* *bring* her. The* *ones* who* *love* their* *dying* *never* *do.*

variants.text: *(He does not see you — not truly; his wide eyes are fixed on a green dark eighty years gone — but as your cold falls over him his thrashing slows, just slightly, the way a drowning man stills when something cool touches his face. The words come out of him in broken gulps, the same words, the words he has said ten thousand times.)* …*can't* — can't *find* — *which* *way's* *UP* — *(he claws at nothing)* — the *boat*, I got the *boat* *in*, all of 'em *safe*, just the *dog*, the *little* *un's* *DOG* went over and she *cried* so I — *(he gulps)* — *cold*, it's so *COLD*, the *water's* in my *BOOTS*, pulling, I can't — *which* way is — *(his chest heaves)* — there's *light*, up *there*, *green* *light*, I just have to — *(he reaches up, and cannot rise)* — *can't* *REACH* it, can't, the *cold's* got my *legs*, I'm — *(a wet, frightened whisper)* — am I — is this — *help* — *somebody* — I don't want to — *not* like *this*, not *down* in the *cold* *dark* *alone* — *(and then it begins again)* …*can't* — can't *find* — *which* *way's* *UP* —*

nodes.speaker: Old Tam
nodes.text: *(Your cold holds him a half-breath out of the loop — long enough that his eyes almost focus on you, a drowning man catching sight of a shape on the surface.)* …*you* — *(he gasps)* — you're *not* *water* — you're *COLD* but you're not — *who* — *are* you the — did you *come* *DOWN* for me? *(desperate, clutching)* *Can* you — *can* you get me *UP?* *Please* — I'll *do* — just get me to the *AIR*, I can't, I can't find which way—*

choices.text: *(read the loop — what exactly is he trapped in?)* "Easy, Tam. Easy. Let me see where you are."
"Tam, listen — the water's gone. It's been gone eighty years. You're not in the flood. You got out. You're remembering it, not living it."

*(the one who crossed and came back)* "Tam — I know exactly where you are, because I've been to the other side of it. You're not drowning. You drowned — once, eighty years ago, brave, going back for a frightened child's dog after you'd saved every soul in your boat. It's *over*, Tam. The water closed and then it was finished, and you crossed, and the worst thing that will ever happen to you already happened and is *done*. You're not in the cold dark. You're in a warm sick-house, and a nurse has tended you eighty years, and there's air — all the air there is. You only have to believe you've surfaced. Because you have."

*(don't argue with the water — breathe him through it; talk him into one slow surfacing breath until his body believes it)* "Tam. Tam. Look at me, not the dark. We're going up together — slow — kick once, reach, and breathe with me. Now. Breathe."
"Hold on, Tam. I'm not leaving you down there. Let me see to the others, then I'll get you up."

nodes.speaker: Old Tam
nodes.text: *(You let your cold sink into the loop and read its shape, and it is plain and terrible: not the whole drowning, but one slice of it, the last twenty seconds, played over forever — the moment after he knew he would not make the surface and before the dark took him, the precise instant of *I am going to die alone down here.*)* *(The reading tells you the lock: Tam is not afraid of dying. He went back for the dog knowing the risk; a brave man made his peace with the danger. What snagged his soul is the word* alone. *He drowned with no hand to hold, no face to see, certain in his last lucid second that he was passing out of the world unwitnessed, unaccompanied, into a cold nobody would share. That is the moment looping — not the water in his lungs, but the* aloneness *of it. He does not need to be pulled up. He needs to not be alone down there — and then to be told the down-there is over.)* *(He thrashes on, gulping his loop — but you know, now, exactly what he is waiting eighty years to hear.)*

nodes.speaker: Old Tam
nodes.text: *(The words reach him, but they slide off the loop like water off oilcloth — he is too deep in the dying to take in a fact that large.)* …*gone?* *(a confused gulp)* …*no* — no, it's *here, it's* in my *BOOTS, it's* — *(he thrashes)* — *don't* — *don't* *TELL* me it's *gone* when I can *FEEL* it, you're — are you *trying* to *trick* me, is this — *(panic spikes)* — *eighty* — what's *eighty* — I don't — there's no *years* down here, there's only *NOW, only* the *cold* and the *green* and *which-way's-up* — *(he slips back under)* …*can't* *find* — *which* *way's* — *(barely, before the loop takes him)* …*you* *have* to *come* *DOWN* to *where* I *AM, cold* *one.* *Don't* *shout* the *truth* at me from the *SURFACE.* I can't — I can't *hear* the *surface* *anymore.* *Come* *DOWN.*

nodes.speaker: Old Tam
nodes.text: *(You don't shout from the surface. You go down to where he is — into the cold and the green and the alone — and you stay there with him, and you tell him it's over from inside the dark, and because you are not shouting from a surface he can't reach but holding his hand at the bottom, he hears you.)* …*over.* *(the thrashing slows)* …*brave.* You — you *said* *brave.* I got the *boat* *in.* All of 'em. *Safe.* *(a shudder)* …I did, didn't I. I *forgot* that. Down here you *forget* the *boat*, you only *remember* the *going-under* — but I — I *saved* 'em *first.* *(his clawing hands go still)* …And you're — you're *DOWN* here *with* me. *(wonderingly)* That's — that's the *thing* I — *eighty* *years* I been *screaming* *'alone, alone, don't* *let* me go *under* *ALONE'* — and *you* *came* *DOWN.* *(his eyes find yours, and at last they focus)* …I'm not *alone* in the *cold.* *(barely)* And if I'm *not* *alone* — and if it's *over* — and if I was *BRAVE* — then I don't — *(he goes quiet, a stillness eighty years denied)* …then I don't have to *find* *which-way's-up* *anymore.* Do I. Because I'm *not* *under.* I'm *out.* I been *out* a *long* *while.* *(he takes a breath — slow, whole, the first in eighty years — and shudders with the wonder of it)* …*Air.* *Gods.* *Air.* *(softly, weeping)* *Thank* you for *coming* *down* for me, cold one. *Same* as I *went* *down* for the *dog.* *Nobody* should go in the *cold* *water* *alone.* *Not* the *dog.* *Not* *me.* *Not* *nobody.*

nodes.speaker: Old Tam
nodes.text: *(You don't fight the water and you don't deny it — you simply become the thing he lost: a calm voice in the dark, counting him up. Kick. Reach. Now breathe. And your certainty is so steady, so unhurried, so *present*, that his drowning body — eighty years convinced it is sinking — chooses, for the first time, to believe the voice over the water.)* *(He kicks. He reaches. And he breaks a surface that has not been there for eighty years — because you told him it was, and meant it, and he trusted you more than he trusted his own terror.)* …*HAH* — *(a vast, whooping, living breath, the gasp of a man broaching into air)* — *HAH* — *AIR* — *I'm* — *(he sits up on the pallet, streaming with water no one else can see, eyes wide and clear and ALIVE with relief)* — *I'm* *UP!* I'm — *cold* *one, I* *BROKE* the *SURFACE, I* — *(he laughs, a drowning man's disbelieving laugh, eighty years overdue)* — you *COUNTED* me *UP!* *'Kick, reach, breathe'* — *plain* as my *old* *da* *teaching* me to *swim* in the *mill-pond* — and I — I *BELIEVED* you, and the *believing* *DID* it! *(he gulps the air greedily, joyously)* *Eighty* *years* *down* and you *talked* me *UP* in *six* *words!* *(he grips your arm, weeping and grinning)* *Bless* your *cold* *steady* *voice, friend. You'd* have made a *ferryman.* *You* *get* *folk* *across.*

nodes.speaker: Old Tam
nodes.text: *(You hold your voice steady and count him up — kick, reach, breathe — and though the loop fights you, your calm is a rope he can climb, and rung by rung his thrashing eases into something slower, something that remembers what a breath is for.)* …*slow* — *(a shaky inhale)* — *slow*, you *said* — *(another)* — I'm — the *cold's* not — it's not *pulling* so *HARD* now — *(his chest rises, falls, rises)* …*am* I — *(a long, trembling breath that holds)* …I think — I think I *got* my *head* *up*, cold one. *(wonderingly)* Not — not all the *way.* I can still *feel* the *water* round my *legs.* But my *FACE* is — my *face* is in the *AIR.* *(he breathes, careful, like a man not trusting the reprieve)* …*Stay* a *moment.* *Keep* *counting.* I — I lose it if you *stop, I* *slip* back *under* — but with your *voice* I can *hold* the *surface.* *(barely)* *Eighty* *years* and this is the *most* *air* I've *had.* *Don't* — *don't* *go* *far.* I'm *nearly* *up.* I just need — I need to *believe* the *rest* of the *way.*

nodes.speaker: Old Tam
nodes.text: *(You reach for the calm certain voice — but your own grip on him slips, the count comes a beat too fast, a shade too anxious, and a drowning man can hear fear the way he hears water. Your unsteadiness tells him the surface isn't safe, and he flinches back down into the green.)* …*you* — *(a panicked gulp)* — you don't *KNOW* the way *up* either, do you, I can *HEAR* it, you're — you're *guessing* — *(he thrashes harder)* — *don't* *GUESS* at a *drowning* *man, cold* *one, don't* — *(he slips under)* …*can't* — *which* *way's* — *(barely, before the loop closes)* …*come* *back* when your *own* *voice* is *steady.* I can't *climb* a *shaking* *rope.* I been *down* *eighty* *years; I* can *wait* for you to *find* your *feet.* But don't — don't *count* me *up* *frightened.* It *frightens* me *worse.*

nodes.speaker: Old Tam
nodes.text: *(Reaching for reassurance, you land on precisely the wrong words — bright and brisk, the way one jollies a child: "There now, nothing to it, just a bit of water, up you pop!" — and to a soul eighty years certain it is dying, *nothing to it* is the cruelest sound in the world.)* …*NOTHING* — *(he rears back, stricken)* — *nothing* *TO* it?! I am *DROWNING*, cold one, I am *DYING* in the *COLD* and the *DARK* and you — *'up* you *POP'?!* *(he thrashes, wounded more than frightened now)* …*(and then, oddly, the sheer wrongness of it snags something in him — a flicker of the old ferryman who suffered fools across his boat for forty years)* …*gods.* *'Up* you *pop.'* *(a strangled, incredulous almost-laugh through the panic)* …*Forty* *years* I *rowed* the *crossings* and I'd have *PUT* a *man* *OVERBOARD* for *saying* *'up* you *pop'* to a *soul* in the *water.* *(he's still drowning — but he's looking at you now, indignant, present)* …*Don't.* *Don't* *jolly* me. *Come* *back* and talk to me like a *man* who *knows* the *cold* is *REAL.* I'll *hear* *that.* I'll *never* *hear* *'nothing* *to* it.* *Now* *let* me — *(he slips, grumbling, back toward the loop)* — *'up* you *POP', he* says, the* *cold* *daft* — *honestly* —*

nodes.speaker: Old Tam
nodes.text: *(Your cold lifts from him, and at once the loop begins to close back over, the green dark rising — but something of your presence lingers, a coolness on his brow, and his thrashing is a shade less wild than before.)* …*don't* — *(a gulp)* — don't be *long*, cold one. *(barely)* I *believe* you'll *come* *back.* That's — that's *more* than I've *had* in *eighty* *years, the* *believing* *someone's* *coming.* *(he slips toward the water)* …*see* to the *others, aye.* The *burning* *lass* — *Maelis* — *(a flicker of the kind old ferryman surfaces through the panic)* — *mind* her *gentle, cold* one. I *hear* her, *nights — 'I've* *got* you, *go' — over* and *over.* *(softly, sinking)* …Whatever's *got* *ME* down here, *hers* is *worse, because* *I* *want* *UP* and *she* — she *don't.* She's *holding* *something* down there in the *fire.* *(barely, as the loop takes him)* …*A* *body* will *drown* *itself* *forever* to *keep* *holding* the *right* *thing.* …*which* *way's* — *can't* *find* —*

conversations.title: Maelis, Who Will Not Leave the Fire

variants.text: *(She does not thrash like the ferryman. She is the stillest soul in the lazaret — curled on her pallet around an emptiness she cradles with infinite care, her face turned down to it, golden firelight that isn't there flickering over her, her lips moving in the same soft litany without end.)* …*it's* *alright.* *I've* *got* you. *I've* *got* you, *love.* *(she shifts the nothing in her arms, gentling it)* …*don't* *look* at the *smoke, look* at *ME, keep* your *eyes* on *Mam — there's* the *window, see* the *window, Tomkin's* *got* his *arms* *up* for you — (her* *voice* *never* *rises, never* *breaks, a* *mother* *holding* *calm* so* a* *child* won't* *fright)* …*one*, *two* — *out* you go — *THERE* — *(a breath of pure relief)* …*good* *girl.* *Good* *girl. You're* *out. You're* *safe.* *(and then her arms, empty, curl back to her chest, and the litany begins again, and the beam has not yet fallen, and never will, because she will not let the moment reach it)* …*it's* *alright.* *I've* *got* you. *I've* *got* you, *love* —*

nodes.speaker: Maelis
nodes.text: *(Your cold settles over her, and her litany pauses — she looks up, and unlike the others her eyes are clear and present and entirely sane, which is somehow worse.)* …*Oh.* *(softly)* A *cold* one. *(she does not let go of the emptiness)* …You're the *first* to *come* who *isn't* *Sister* *Maud.* *(a small, tired, knowing smile)* …She *frets* over me. *Tries* to *coax* me *up.* *Bless* her, she *doesn't* *understand.* *(she strokes the nothing in her arms)* …You can *speak*, if you *like.* But I'll *tell* you *plain*, cold one, before you *start*: I *know* what you've *come* to *say. And I'm *not* *leaving.* *Not* this. *Not* *her.*

choices.text: *(read what holds her — gently)* "May I just look, Maelis? I'll not touch her. I only want to see what you're holding."
"Tell me about her, Maelis. Your daughter. The one in your arms."
"You're reliving the worst moment of your life over and over, Maelis. The fire. The beam coming. Why hold yourself in it — why not step free of the burning?"

*(the one who knows what a soul truly keeps)* "Maelis — listen, because I crossed over and came back, and I can see the thing the fire hides from you. You think this moment is where you lose her. It isn't. Look again — she goes out the window, into Tomkin's arms, *'good girl, you're out, you're safe.'* This is the moment you *saved* her. Your last act in the world wasn't dying. It was *winning* — getting her out, alive. And she *stayed* alive, Maelis. She grew up. Grew old. Died warm in a bed sixty years on with grandchildren in the room, because of *this*, because of what you did here. You don't need the fire to keep her. The fire's not where she is. She's *out the window* — saved — and you can carry *that* forever instead of the burning."
"I'll not rush you, Maelis. Hold her a while. I'll see to the others."

nodes.speaker: Maelis
nodes.text: *(She tips the emptiness in her arms toward you, trusting, and you let your cold read the loop — and find, to your surprise, that it is not a loop of grief at all. It is a loop of *triumph*, held so tightly it has curdled into a cage.)* *(The reading is clear: Maelis is not trapped in her death. She is trapped in her one perfect victory. The instant she loops is the instant the child cleared the window into safe hands — the single best thing she ever did, the moment a mother's whole love resolved into one act that *worked*. She has not stayed for the fire. She has stayed for the four seconds of pure rightness just before the beam, when she knew — knew beyond doubting — that her girl would live and she had made it so. She replays it not to suffer, but because it is the only place left where she gets to save her daughter again. To leave the loop feels, to her, like letting the child fall. She does not need rescuing from a horror. She needs to learn that the victory is permanent — that stepping out won't un-save the girl, because the girl was saved for good, sixty years ago, and stays saved whether Maelis watches or not.)*

nodes.speaker: Maelis
nodes.text: *(Her whole face softens, and for a moment the firelight in it is just warmth.)* …*Her* *name's* *Bell.* *(she smiles down at the emptiness)* *Annabel.* *Six* — *(a flicker)* — *six* when the *fire* *came.* *Stubborn* as a *goat* and *twice* as *loud, and* she had this — this *LAUGH, cold* one, this *enormous* *laugh* for *such* a *scrap* of a *thing, you'd* hear it *three* *crofts* *over.* *(she strokes the nothing)* …I *can't* *quite* *hear* it *now.* That's the *one* *cruelty.* In *here* I *get* her *WEIGHT* — the *exact* *weight* of her in my *arms, six* *years* of *girl* — and her *little* *voice* *saying* *'Mam* *I'm* *SCARED' and* me *saying* *'I've* *got* you' — but the *LAUGH* I — *(her brow furrows)* — I can't *reach* the *laugh* from *inside* the *smoke.* The *smoke* only *holds* the *scared* *part.* *(she shakes her head, clearing it)* …*No* *matter.* I *have* her *WEIGHT.* That's *more* than *most* *grieving* *mothers* *get — to* *hold* the* *weight* of* their* *child* *forever.* *(fiercely tender)* I'll not *complain* of *missing* the *laugh* when I've *got* the *WEIGHT.* *Some* *mothers* *get* *neither.* *(she looks at you)* …Did *you* *have* a *Mam, cold* one, who'd have *gone* *into* a *fire* for you? *(softly)* *Everyone* *should.* *Everyone* *should* have* *someone* who'd* *go* *into* the* *fire.*

nodes.speaker: Maelis
nodes.text: *(For the first time a hardness comes into her, the iron under the gentle.)* …*Why* *not* *step* *free.* *(quietly)* …*Because* *'free'* is a *place* *without* *her*, cold one. *(she holds the emptiness closer)* *Everyone* *asks* it like the *answer's* *obvious.* *'Poor* *Maelis, trapped* in* the* *fire, won't* *someone* *coax* her* *OUT.'* *(her voice sharpens)* But *OUT* is *worse.* I *know* what's *out* there. I *died* *sixty* *years* before my *Bell* did — she *grew* *up* *without* me, *cold* one, *married, had* her* *own* *babes, grew* *OLD, and* I — I *missed* *ALL* of it, every* *birthday, every* *scraped* *knee, every* — *(her voice catches, then steadies into iron)* …So *don't* tell me to *step* *OUT* into the *empty* *years* where my *girl* is *grown* and *gone* and I *wasn't* *THERE.* *(barely)* *In* *here, she's* *six.* *In* *here, she* *needs* me, and I'm *ENOUGH* for her, I can *SAVE* her, I'm the *whole* *world* to her and the *fire's* the *only* *place* *left* where *that's* *TRUE.* *(fiercely)* You call it the *worst* *moment* of my *life. It* *isn't.* It's the *LAST* *moment* I *got* to *be* her *Mam.* And I will *hold* it — *burning* — *forever* — before I *step* *out* into *sixty* *years* of *being* *nobody's* *mother* in the *cold.* *(quietly, the iron trembling)* …*Wouldn't* *you?*

nodes.speaker: Maelis
nodes.text: *(She goes still — and for the first time in eighty years she lifts her eyes from the emptiness in her arms and looks, really looks, at the moment around her: the window, the small shape clearing it, the safe hands beyond.)* …*the* *moment* I — *(barely)* …*saved* her. *(her voice shakes)* Not — not *lost.* *Saved.* *(she stares at the window of fire only she can see)* …*'Good* *girl. You're* *out. You're* *safe.'* *(she breathes)* …I *say* it *every* *time. *Eighty* *years.* And I never — I *heard* it as the *goodbye.* The *last* *thing* before the *beam.* But it's *not* a *goodbye*, is it. It's a — *(she presses a hand to her mouth)* — it's a *VICTORY.* It's the *sound* of a *mother* who *WON.* *(tears, but her face is changing)* …And she *lived.* You *said* she — *(she grips your arm)* — *grew* *old.* *Warm* *bed.* *Grandbabes* in the *room.* *Sixty* *years* of *LAUGH* — the *laugh* I couldn't *reach* in the *smoke — she* *got* to *keep* it, *spend* it, *give* it to *HER* *babes — because* of *THIS.* Because of the *window.* Because of *'one, two, out* you* *go.'* *(she looks down at her empty arms, and slowly, slowly, understands)* …I don't *keep* her by *holding* the *fire.* *(barely)* The *fire's* not *where* she *is.* She's *OUT* the *window.* She's *SIXTY* *years* of *life* I *bought* her with the *last* *thing* I *ever* *did.* *(she rises from the pallet, and the firelight gutters out around her, and she is not burning anymore)* …I can *carry* *THAT.* The *winning.* The *window.* *'Good* *girl, you're* *safe.'* *(she weeps, and smiles, and it is the smile of a soul setting down something it has carried eighty years and finding its arms were never empty after all)* …I was *her* *Mam* to the *very* *last* *second, cold* one, and the *last* *second* was a *RESCUE.* That's — *(barely)* — that's the *best* thing a *body* can *be.* I'll *take* *that* *out* into the *cold* *years.* And do you *know* — *(softly, wondering)* — I think the *laugh* might be *out* *there.* *Waiting.* I think you only* *can't* *hear* it from* *inside* the* *smoke.*

nodes.speaker: Maelis
nodes.text: *(She nods, grateful, and curls back around the emptiness she cradles, her litany already beginning again under her breath.)* …*Thank* you. *(softly)* For not — for not *coaxing.* *(she gentles the nothing in her arms)* …*Everyone* *coaxes.* *Sister* *Maud* *coaxes.* As though I'm a *fool* who *wandered* *into* a *fire* and *can't* find the *door.* *(a tired smile)* I *know* where the *door* is, cold one. I *stand* *in* the *fire* on *purpose.* *(she looks down at her arms)* …*Go* *on.* *See* to *Tam — the* *poor* *drowning* *soul, he* *WANTS* his *door, give* him *yours* *first.* *(barely, as the litany takes her)* …*Me, I'll* *be* *here.* *Holding.* *It's* *alright.* *I've* *got* you. *I've* *got* you, *love* — *(she pauses, just once, and glances up)* …*Though* — cold one — *if* you *find* you've a *thing* to *say* that *isn't* *coaxing* — a *thing* I* *haven't* *heard* — *(quietly)* …*come* *back.* I've *held* this *eighty* *years.* My *arms* are — *(she falters)* …*no.* *Never* *mind.* *Go* on. *I've* *got* her. I've *got* her.*
